mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and supporting economic growth in the current economic scenario.
Analyze the impact of the RBI's monetary policy decisions on various sectors of the Indian economy.
Evaluate the effectiveness of inflation targeting as a monetary policy framework in India.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI emblem with a graph showing stable interest rates against a backdrop of economic indicators.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils Comprehensive National Cyber Security Strategy 2025
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) unveiled the 'National Cyber Security Strategy 2025' aimed at safeguarding India's digital infrastructure.
• The strategy focuses on enhancing national cyber resilience, strengthening critical information infrastructure protection, and fostering a culture of cyber hygiene.
• Key pillars include robust threat intelligence sharing, capacity building in cyber forensics, international cooperation, and public-private partnerships.
• It also emphasizes skill development and R&D in emerging cyber technologies to counter sophisticated cyber threats.
• The strategy is crucial for protecting citizens' data, securing digital transactions, and ensuring the uninterrupted functioning of essential services in the digital age.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
GS3.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: National Cyber Security Strategy aims to protect India's digital space.
Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) refers to vital digital assets.
MeitY is the nodal ministry for cyber security in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats and their implications for India's national security and economic stability.
Critically analyze the key provisions and implementation challenges of the National Cyber Security Strategy 2025.
Suggest measures to strengthen India's cyber security framework, including technological advancements, legal reforms, and international cooperation.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract digital network lines forming a shield, representing cyber security and protection.
GS3.trivia: The first computer virus, 'Creeper', appeared in 1971, designed to move between ARPANET hosts.

GS3.headline: Centre Announces New Policy for Sustainable Urban Waste Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs unveiled a new national policy aimed at transforming urban waste management practices across India.
• The policy emphasizes a circular economy approach, focusing on waste reduction, reuse, recycling, and energy recovery, moving away from landfills.
• Key interventions include mandatory source segregation, promotion of decentralized waste processing units, and incentivizing private sector participation.
• It also outlines stricter enforcement mechanisms for waste management rules and capacity building for urban local bodies.
• The initiative is critical for addressing growing urban waste challenges, improving public health, and contributing to India's environmental sustainability goals.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: New policy focuses on waste reduction, reuse, recycling, and energy recovery.
Mandatory source segregation is a key component.
Aims to move away from landfill-centric waste management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges associated with urban solid waste management in India and its environmental and public health implications.
Analyze the key features of the new national policy for sustainable urban waste management and its potential to bring about systemic change.
Suggest innovative approaches and best practices from around the world that India can adopt for effective waste management.
GS3.imageDescription: Garbage bins with segregation labels (wet, dry, recyclable) in an urban setting, symbolizing proper waste management.
GS3.trivia: India generates approximately 1.5 lakh metric tonnes of solid waste every day, with a significant portion remaining untreated.

GS3.headline: Private Sector Investment Surges in Renewable Energy Sector, Driven by Policy Support
GS3.explainer:
• India's renewable energy sector witnessed a significant surge in private sector investment, driven by consistent government policy support and attractive incentives.
• The investment inflow is primarily directed towards solar, wind, and green hydrogen projects, reflecting confidence in India's clean energy transition.
• Factors contributing to this growth include production-linked incentive (PLI) schemes, simplified approval processes, and robust power purchase agreements.
• This surge is crucial for India to meet its ambitious renewable energy targets and reduce its reliance on fossil fuels.
• The trend indicates a positive outlook for achieving energy security and combating climate change through sustainable development.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Government policies like PLI schemes are boosting renewable energy investment.
Solar, wind, and green hydrogen are key areas of investment.
Increased renewable capacity contributes to energy security and climate goals.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of government policies and incentives in attracting private investment in India's renewable energy sector.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of accelerating renewable energy adoption in India.
Examine the challenges that still persist in scaling up renewable energy infrastructure and suggest policy interventions to overcome them.
GS3.imageDescription: Solar panels and wind turbines against a clear sky, symbolizing renewable energy growth.
GS3.trivia: India is the third-largest energy consuming country in the world and has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

GS3.headline: AI-Powered Crop Monitoring System Piloted in Several States to Boost Agricultural Productivity
GS3.explainer:
• An advanced Artificial Intelligence (AI)-powered crop monitoring and advisory system has been successfully piloted in select districts across multiple states.
• The system uses satellite imagery, drone data, and weather forecasts to provide real-time insights on crop health, pest infestations, and irrigation needs to farmers.
• Developed in collaboration with agricultural universities and tech firms, it aims to enhance precision agriculture and optimize resource utilization.
• Early results indicate significant improvements in yield prediction, reduction in input costs, and timely intervention for crop protection.
• The initiative is part of a broader push towards digital agriculture, empowering farmers with data-driven decisions to increase productivity and income.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.

Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
prelimsPoints: AI is being used for crop monitoring and advisory services.
System uses satellite imagery, drone data, and weather forecasts.
Aims to improve yield prediction and reduce input costs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of Artificial Intelligence and other emerging technologies to revolutionize Indian agriculture and address challenges like climate change and food security.
Analyze the benefits and challenges of implementing AI-powered crop monitoring systems for small and marginal farmers in India.
Evaluate the role of government initiatives and public-private partnerships in promoting digital agriculture and empowering farmers.
GS3.imageDescription: A drone flying over green fields, capturing data for crop monitoring, with digital overlays.
GS3.trivia: India is the second-largest producer of rice and wheat in the world, and agriculture is the primary source of livelihood for about 58% of its population.

GS3.headline: Discovery of New Species Highlights Rich Biodiversity of Western Ghats
GS3.explainer:
• Scientists announced the discovery of a new species of frog in the Western Ghats, further underscoring the region's immense biodiversity.
• The newly identified amphibian, characterized by its unique vocalization and genetic markers, was found during a recent ecological survey.
• The Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity, home to thousands of endemic species.
• This discovery emphasizes the urgent need for continued conservation efforts and protection of critical habitats facing threats from deforestation and climate change.
• Research on new species contributes vital data for understanding evolutionary processes and implementing effective biodiversity management strategies.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Western Ghats is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a biodiversity hotspot.
It is known for its high endemism.
Discovery of new species highlights the region's ecological importance.
GS3.imageDescription: A close-up photograph of the newly discovered frog species in its natural habitat in the Western Ghats.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats mountain range is older than the Himalayas, and its forests are home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

GS3.headline: India-Pakistan Border Talks Conclude with Commitment to Maintain Ceasefire
GS3.explainer:
• High-level border talks between India and Pakistan concluded with both sides reaffirming their commitment to upholding the ceasefire agreement along the Line of Control (LoC).
• The discussions, held at the sector commander level, focused on resolving local issues, preventing inadvertent escalations, and enhancing communication channels.
• While no major breakthrough was reported on broader political issues, the agreement to maintain peace along the border is seen as a positive step.
• The talks are part of ongoing efforts to de-escalate tensions and ensure stability in the sensitive border regions.
• Both nations emphasized the importance of existing mechanisms for addressing grievances and maintaining peace and tranquility.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
India and its neighborhood- relations.
prelimsPoints: Line of Control (LoC) divides Indian and Pakistani controlled parts of Jammu & Kashmir.
Ceasefire agreement aims to maintain peace along the LoC.
Border talks are held between military officials of both countries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of maintaining peace and tranquility along the Line of Control for India's national security and regional stability.
Analyze the persistent challenges in India-Pakistan relations and the role of dialogue in de-escalating tensions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of various confidence-building measures in managing border disputes between India and Pakistan.
GS3.imageDescription: Indian and Pakistani military officers shaking hands across a symbolic border line, with flags in the background.
GS3.trivia: The Line of Control (LoC) is a de facto boundary, not a legally recognized international border, that emerged from the 1948 and 1971 conflicts.

sources: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
